refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Document operator mode (direct vs. tele-operated), task complexity, and any safety/policy constraints that affected performance.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall provide sufficient power, speed, and bucket/blade capacity to execute earthmoving tasks at the required throughput rate.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (direct or remote) shall enable precise, responsive control of vehicle motion and work equipment with minimal latency or cognitive load.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The teleoperation system shall deliver real-time situational awareness (video, sensor data, vehicle state) to the remote operator with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity parity with on-site operation.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The safety services system shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without imposing productivity penalties that would prevent achievement of productivity_target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-power, high-speed earthmoving vehicle with optimized bucket/blade geometry and operator control responsiveness; couple with a low-latency teleoperation system that streams high-fidelity video and sensor telemetry; enforce safety constraints via real-time monitoring and intervention.

DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle capability (power, speed, work equipment) and operator effectiveness (control interface, situational awareness); design vehicle subsystem for maximum throughput; design teleoperation subsystem for minimal latency and cognitive load; design safety subsystem to operate in parallel without blocking task execution.

DP-C: Achieve productivity target through a modular architecture where vehicle performance is decoupled from operator interface and safety enforcement; use standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) to enable independent optimization of each subsystem and graceful degradation under safety constraints.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Achieve productivity target through a modular architecture where vehicle performance is decoupled from operator interface and safety enforcement; use standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) to enable independent optimization of each subsystem and graceful degradation under safety constraints.

potential_sources: Vehicle power/speed design must be validated against operator control latency and safety intervention response time to ensure productivity is not bottlenecked by control loop delays.
Safety constraint enforcement (SafetyAlert) must not introduce blocking delays that prevent task execution; safety and productivity must be balanced via policy, not architectural coupling.
Teleoperation latency (Telemetry round-trip) must be characterized and acceptable to operator; if latency exceeds operator cognitive model, productivity will degrade regardless of vehicle capability.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits low coupling because it explicitly separates concerns via standardized interfaces. However, integration testing is required to validate that the three subsystems (vehicle, teleoperation, safety) operate harmoniously under representative workload. The decoupling is logical; physical/temporal coupling must be verified.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator skill and training are held constant across on-site and remote modes; if remote operators require significantly more training, productivity parity may not be achievable.

Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies can be expressed as constraints that do not fundamentally conflict with productivity_target; if policies impose hard limits on vehicle speed or work envelope, target may be unachievable.

Risk: Teleoperation latency may exceed operator cognitive model (typically 200–500 ms for heavy equipment); if so, remote productivity will be lower than on-site, and target may be unachievable in remote mode.
Risk: Safety intervention (e.g., emergency stop, geofence enforcement) may introduce unpredictable delays that reduce effective task time and lower productivity below target.
Risk: Vehicle power/speed design may be over-constrained by safety policies (e.g., speed limits in proximity to personnel), reducing throughput below target.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly state acceptable latency bounds for Telemetry and Tasking interfaces, and define safety intervention response time requirements. Add a sub-DP for graceful degradation: if safety constraints are active, define the minimum acceptable productivity level and operator notification strategy.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It is responsible for orchestrating task execution, managing inter-subsystem communication via standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link), and ensuring that productivity targets are achieved while maintaining safety and policy compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle and safety system state (position, orientation, speed, bucket load, safety status, alerts) streamed to operator interface and monitoring systems. Frequency and fidelity must support operator situational awareness and safety decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (move, dig, dump, emergency stop) and task parameters (target location, work envelope, priority) received from on-site or remote operator interface. Latency and reliability must support responsive control.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety events (geofence violation, personnel proximity, equipment fault, policy violation) generated by Safety Services and communicated to operator and monitoring systems. Must trigger appropriate operator notification and/or automatic intervention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for teleoperation mode, connecting on-site vehicle and safety systems to remote operator and monitoring infrastructure. Latency, bandwidth, and reliability are critical to productivity and safety.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level boundary for this FR. It owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and is responsible for orchestrating productivity achievement. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary. This is the correct assignment.

Vehicle subsystem owns power, speed, and work equipment design, which are necessary but not sufficient for productivity. Operator interface, teleoperation, and safety enforcement are outside Vehicle scope. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for operator effectiveness and safety integration, leading to incomplete solution.

Teleoperation subsystem owns remote operator interface and situational awareness, which are necessary for remote productivity. However, it does not own vehicle capability or safety enforcement. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would ignore on-site operation mode and vehicle performance constraints.

Safety Services owns policy enforcement and constraint management, which are necessary to ensure productivity is achieved safely. However, it does not own vehicle capability or operator interface. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services alone would be a category error: safety is an enabler, not the primary driver of productivity.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the correct owner of FR-NGHE-001 because it is the only module that spans all three subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and can orchestrate their integration to achieve the productivity target. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that contribute to productivity but do not independently own the requirement.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration module; if it is not given clear authority and interface definitions, responsibility for productivity may become diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, leading to finger-pointing and missed targets.
Risk: Productivity measurement and verification must be owned by NGHE Solution (or a dedicated test/validation module); if ownership is unclear, acceptance criteria may not be rigorously evaluated.

Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are designed independently without explicit productivity constraints, their integration may not achieve the target. NGHE Solution must enforce cross-subsystem constraints (e.g., latency budgets, safety intervention response times).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit ownership and interface contracts: (1) Vehicle subsystem shall deliver power, speed, and work equipment performance to a defined specification; (2) Teleoperation subsystem shall deliver operator interface and situational awareness with latency ≤ X ms; (3) Safety Services shall enforce policies with intervention response time ≤ Y ms; (4) NGHE Solution shall integrate these subsystems and verify that productivity_target is achieved. Define acceptance criteria for each subsystem and for the integrated system.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (modular architecture with standardized interfaces) is a sound logical solution to FR-NGHE-001 (achieve productivity target). The DP decouples vehicle capability, operator interface, and safety enforcement, enabling independent optimization and graceful degradation. The DP is not over-constrained and allows for multiple physical realizations. However, the DP assumes that latency, safety intervention response time, and operator cognitive load can be managed within acceptable bounds; these assumptions must be validated during detailed design and integration testing.

diffusion_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration module; if sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are not tightly coupled via interface contracts, diffusion of responsibility may occur. Mitigation: Define explicit interface specifications (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with latency, bandwidth, and reliability requirements.

Risk: Productivity measurement and verification may be diffused across multiple modules if ownership is not clear. Mitigation: Assign a dedicated test/validation module or team to measure productivity and verify acceptance criteria.

Risk: Safety constraint enforcement may introduce unpredictable delays that reduce productivity. Mitigation: Define safety intervention response time requirements and validate that they do not prevent achievement of productivity_target.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for DP-C. It owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and is responsible for orchestrating productivity achievement. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and necessary for integration. No significant diffusion is expected if interface contracts are enforced.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trials. Productivity can be measured directly (m³/h or ton/h) and compared against productivity_target. Operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task complexity, and safety/policy constraints can be documented and controlled. However, verification requires access to representative worksite conditions and trained operators; this may be a logistical constraint.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Teleoperation, and Safety Services respectively. Each sub-FR is atomic and can be designed and verified independently, with integration testing to validate that the sub-systems work together to achieve the top-level FR.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to include explicit latency budgets for Telemetry and Tasking interfaces, and define safety intervention response time requirements. These are critical to operator effectiveness and productivity.
Define acceptance criteria for each sub-FR (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) and for the integrated system. Specify measurement methods, success criteria, and evaluation conditions.

Establish interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link, including latency, bandwidth, reliability, and format specifications. These contracts are essential to ensure that sub-modules integrate correctly.

Identify and mitigate risks related to operator skill, worksite safety plan conflicts, teleoperation latency, and safety intervention delays. These are the primary threats to achieving productivity_target.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-FRs to be actionable. The refined FR and sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) are well-defined and atomic, and can be allocated to specific subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). DP-C is a sound logical solution that decouples concerns via standardized interfaces. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary. However, the sub-FRs must be further decomposed to define specific design parameters and implementation modules for each subsystem. Proceeding to the next level of decomposition is necessary to drive detailed design and validation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall provide sufficient power, speed, and bucket/blade capacity to execute earthmoving tasks at the required throughput rate.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (direct or remote) shall enable precise, responsive control of vehicle motion and work equipment with minimal latency or cognitive load.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The teleoperation system shall deliver real-time situational awareness (video, sensor data, vehicle state) to the remote operator with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity parity with on-site operation.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The safety services system shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without imposing productivity penalties that would prevent achievement of productivity_target.

assumptions: Operator skill and training are held constant across on-site and remote modes.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies can be expressed as constraints that do not fundamentally conflict with productivity_target.
Teleoperation latency can be managed within acceptable bounds (≤ 500 ms round-trip).
Safety intervention response time can be managed within acceptable bounds (≤ 100 ms).
Vehicle power/speed design is not over-constrained by safety policies.
Productivity measurement can be performed reliably during representative operations trials.
open_risks: Risk: Teleoperation latency may exceed operator cognitive model, reducing remote productivity below on-site levels and preventing achievement of productivity_target in remote mode.
Risk: Safety intervention (e.g., emergency stop, geofence enforcement) may introduce unpredictable delays that reduce effective task time and lower productivity below target.
Risk: Vehicle power/speed design may be over-constrained by safety policies, reducing throughput below target.
Risk: Operator skill and training requirements for remote operation may be significantly higher than for on-site operation, limiting adoption and productivity.
Risk: Worksite safety plan and operational policies may impose hard limits on vehicle speed or work envelope that are incompatible with productivity_target.
Risk: Productivity measurement during trials may not be representative of actual field conditions, leading to acceptance of a system that underperforms in production.
evidence_needed: Latency characterization for Telemetry and Tasking interfaces under representative network conditions (on-site and remote).
Safety intervention response time measurements for geofence, personnel proximity, and policy violation scenarios.
Operator cognitive load assessment for on-site and remote control modes; comparison of operator effectiveness and productivity.
Vehicle power/speed performance data under representative soil and task conditions.
Worksite safety plan and operational policy documentation; analysis of constraints that may impact productivity.
Representative operations trial results, including productivity measurements (m³/h or ton/h), operator mode, task complexity, and safety/policy constraint impacts.
Acceptance criteria and verification plan for FR-NGHE-001 and sub-FRs.
